bullets.text: Iran war spending and disruption risk collides with promised U.S. growth agenda, raising deficits, inflation pressure, and political trade-offs between guns and butter.
IEA announces largest-ever emergency oil stockpile release; crude may still rise if Hormuz risks persist and spare capacity can’t offset sustained disruption.
Market view: oil unlikely to reach $150/barrel as demand destruction, non-OPEC supply, and policy releases cap spikes despite war-risk premiums.
Risk-off rotation: war-driven oil surge hits equities; investors tilt toward energy/defense hedges and diversify across “hated” value stocks outperforming in 2026.
Bitcoin outperforms S&P 500, Nasdaq, and gold since war start, reflecting demand for alternative hedges and speculative liquidity amid geopolitical uncertainty.
Berkshire signals possible share buyback resumption, supporting equity floor and highlighting capital allocation confidence versus elevated valuations elsewhere.
NYC faces ~$7B budget gap; proposals like ending free parking signal politically difficult revenue measures amid rising municipal service and financing costs.
Fertitta in talks to acquire Caesars; a mega-deal could reshape U.S. gaming, leverage up balance sheets, and invite activist pressure with Icahn nearby.
U.S. Army awards Anduril contract up to $20B, accelerating defense-tech procurement and signaling sustained demand for autonomous systems amid great-power competition.
Airbus preparing two uncrewed combat aircraft for European first flights, highlighting rapid militarization of autonomy and Europe’s push to reduce U.S. dependence.
Meta reportedly considers layoffs affecting up to 20%, underscoring cost-cutting, AI-driven restructuring, and a weaker ad cycle sensitivity to macro shocks.
Greenland ice sheet hosts giant hidden swirling plumes, improving models of melt dynamics and future sea-level rise risk relevant to insurers, infrastructure, and sovereign planning.
Cambridge lab error yields powerful new method to modify drug molecules, potentially accelerating medicinal chemistry and reducing R&D iteration costs.
